title: Restored Victorian Villa with Sunny North-Facing Garden in the Heart of Ponsonby
description: Step through the original timber gate and the years melt away: leadlight, ornate fretwork and polished native floors greet you, before the home opens up into a light-flooded modern extension that spills onto a sheltered courtyard. The north-facing garden is a genuine sun-trap, fully fenced for little ones and the dog. With Three Lamps, the boutiques and the buzz of Ponsonby Road just a few minutes on foot, this is character-villa living without compromise.

title: Light-Filled Two-Bedroom Apartment Steps from Ponsonby Central
description: Top-floor and gloriously private, this two-bedroom apartment soaks up sun from morning to night, with treetops and rooftops stretching out toward the city. Slide back the floor-to-ceiling glass and the living room simply becomes the balcony, ideal for a long lunch with friends. Lift access and a secure car park take the hassle out of daily life, and Ponsonby Central, Western Park and a dozen great restaurants are all an easy wander away.

title: Classic Family Bungalow on a Quiet Tree-Lined Street in Mount Eden
description: There's a real sense of homecoming here. High ceilings and original leadlight set the tone inside, where sympathetic updates have kept the 1920s soul intact while adding everyday comfort. Out back, the flat, fully fenced section runs to established fruit trees, with plenty of lawn for cricket or a trampoline. You're zoned for the schools families chase, a few minutes from Mount Eden village, and the summit track is practically your weekend backyard.

title: Architecturally Designed Townhouse with Double Glazing in Mount Eden
description: Designed to be lived in, not laboured over, this near-new townhouse stacks three considered levels around a sun-soaked top-floor living space that opens to a sheltered deck. The scullery keeps the kitchen pristine, the master suite hides a generous walk-in robe, and double glazing means quiet, warm nights. Dominion Road's noodle bars and cafes are minutes away, the city is a quick hop, and Maungawhau's green flank looms reassuringly close.

title: Charming Double-Bay Villa with Off-Street Parking in Grey Lynn
description: A white picket fence, two proud bay windows and a wide central hall set the scene for this much-loved villa. Sash windows and ornate ceilings nod to its 1910 origins, while a thoughtful rear addition brings in light and connects to a deck and level lawn that beg for a summer barbecue. The location is pure Grey Lynn: a stroll to the park, the Sunday farmers' market and the quirky cafes and shops of West Lynn.

title: Stylish Low-Maintenance Townhouse Moments from Grey Lynn Park
description: Lock it, leave it, and get on with your weekend. This freehold townhouse is built for people with better things to do than mow lawns, pairing a crisp, easy-care interior with seamless flow to a courtyard that catches the afternoon sun. A heat pump and full insulation keep things snug through winter, while Williamson Avenue's coffee and brunch crowd is a short stroll from your door. Downsizers and busy professionals, this one's for you.

title: Grand Family Residence with Pool and City Glimpses in Remuera
description: Built for a household that loves to gather, this substantial brick-and-cedar residence rises from a sunny elevated site with formal and casual living zones that flow effortlessly to a wide entertaining terrace. The in-ground pool sparkles beyond manicured gardens, and on a clear day the city skyline winks back at you. Firmly inside the double grammar zone, with Remuera village and Newmarket's shopping close at hand, it delivers scale and address in equal measure.

title: Elegant Brick Character Home in a Premier Remuera Pocket
description: Some homes simply age with grace, and this 1930s brick beauty is one of them. French doors open to established north-facing grounds, picture rails and a feature fireplace anchor the living spaces, and the gracious proportions feel as relevant today as they did ninety years ago. Kitchen and bathrooms have been tastefully renovated so nothing's left to do but move in. Remuera Road's boutiques, cafes and the train station are an easy walk away.

title: Modern Waterfront Apartment with Sea Views in Takapuna
description: Imagine your morning coffee as the sun lifts over Rangitoto and light spills across the Hauraki Gulf. From the wide balcony of this immaculate, fully furnished apartment, that's just Tuesday. Open-plan living frames the water, the building looks after you with a lift, gym and basement parking, and Takapuna Beach plus the cafes and boutiques of Hurstmere Road are a short stroll from the lobby. Move in, unpack, exhale.

title: Sun-Soaked Coastal Family Home a Short Walk to Takapuna Beach
description: Tucked away on a quiet cul-de-sac, this updated weatherboard home does relaxed beachside living properly. Multiple living areas mean the family can spread out yet still come together, and they all open to a private, fully fenced garden where the kids and dog can run free. The sunny deck is made for those long, lingering summer evenings. Takapuna Beach, the Lake Pupuke walkway and the town centre's buzz are all within easy reach.

title: Character Seaside Cottage with Harbour Outlook in Historic Devonport
description: On one of Devonport's most sought-after streets, this restored cottage wears its seaside charm with quiet confidence. A crackling fireplace warms the cosy interior, period details remain intact, and the rear garden is a proper sun-trap for lazy weekend reading. The village and ferry are a short walk away, and when you fancy a view, Mount Victoria delivers a sweeping harbour-and-city panorama just up the road. Downtown is barely twelve minutes by boat.

title: Spacious Brick-and-Tile Family Home in a Popular Howick School Zone
description: Solid, sensible and surprisingly roomy, this brick-and-tile home keeps everything on one easy level, ideal for families and anyone over the stairs. The big open-plan living area rolls out to a covered patio and a flat backyard the kids will claim as their own, while ample off-street parking sorts the boat, trailer or third car. Walk to Howick village, the beach reserve and a clutch of well-regarded local schools.

title: Brand-New Freehold Townhouse with Double Glazing in Papakura
description: Keys in hand, nothing to fix, nothing to wait for, this freehold townhouse is finished and ready for its first owners. Double glazing and full insulation mean it's warm and dry from day one, the open-plan ground floor opens to a fenced courtyard, and upstairs three bedrooms include a master with its own ensuite. With the motorway, train station and Papakura's fast-growing town centre all close by, the commute and the weekend both just got easier.

title: Sun-Drenched Villa Walk to Ponsonby Road
description: Behind a classic white picket fence sits this character-rich villa with soaring stud heights and polished native timber floors. The renovated kitchen flows to a north-facing deck and a low-maintenance courtyard garden ideal for weekend entertaining. You're a five-minute stroll from the cafes, boutiques and buzz of Ponsonby Road, with Three Lamps and the city beyond. Viewings are by appointment and this one won't sit on the market long, so come prepared.

title: Stylish Furnished Apartment in the Heart of Ponsonby
description: This sleek one-bedroom apartment pairs an open-plan living zone with a juliet balcony catching the afternoon light. Fully furnished and move-in ready, it suits a professional wanting walkable access to Ponsonby Central and Western Park. Secure entry and a designated car park complete the package. Bring a bag and a toothbrush; everything else is already here, right down to the kettle.

title: Renovated Bungalow Near Mount Eden Village
description: A warm 1920s bungalow that has been thoughtfully modernised while keeping its leadlight windows and original fireplaces. The sunny living room opens to a private rear lawn fringed by established natives, and the volcanic slopes of Maungawhau are at the end of the street. Walk to Mount Eden Village for the farmers market and grammar zone schooling. Four bedrooms and double-zone schools make this one squarely aimed at families putting down roots.

title: Modern Three-Level Townhouse in Mount Eden
description: Crisp and contemporary, this freehold-feel townhouse delivers three levels of light-filled living with a designer kitchen at its core. The top-floor master enjoys a walk-in wardrobe and ensuite, while the ground floor opens to a fenced courtyard. Double glazing and a heat pump keep things snug year-round. The Dominion Road buses are a block away, so a car-light tenant could happily get by without ever joining the motorway queue.

title: Character Cottage on a Quiet Grey Lynn Street
description: Tucked away on a leafy no-exit street, this cosy two-bedroom cottage charms with its tongue-and-groove ceilings and sunny breakfast nook. A compact but productive vege garden and a sheltered deck make outdoor living easy. You're moments from Grey Lynn Park, Richmond Road eateries and the western train line. It's a small footprint, so honest about storage, but the location and warmth more than make up for it.

title: Bright Two-Bedroom Apartment Close to Grey Lynn Cafes
description: Light pours into this second-floor apartment thanks to a wall of double-glazed windows framing leafy treetops. The open kitchen and living area extend onto a covered balcony perfect for morning coffee. With secure parking and a storage cage included, it's a tidy lock-up-and-leave for two. Pet-friendly by negotiation, and the body corporate is refreshingly low-drama by inner-west standards.

title: Spacious Family Home in Prized Remuera Double Grammar Zone
description: Set on an elevated section, this substantial weatherboard home offers generous formal and casual living spaces that flow to a sun-soaked entertainer's deck. Four double bedrooms include a master with ensuite and walk-in wardrobe, and the leafy garden is fully fenced for children and pets. Positioned in the coveted double grammar zone, with Remuera village and the train station an easy walk away. References and a stable tenancy history will matter to the owners here, who plan to lease long-term.

title: Tidy Two-Bedroom Unit in Leafy Remuera
description: This well-kept brick-and-tile unit sits within a small, quiet block close to Upland Road shops. Both bedrooms are doubles, the lounge is warmed by a heat pump, and a single garage adds handy storage. A small private patio catches the afternoon sun. For anyone chasing a Remuera address without a Remuera mortgage, this is about as sensible as it gets.

title: Near-New Townhouse Steps from Takapuna Beach
description: Coastal living meets modern convenience in this immaculate townhouse just a short walk from the golden sand of Takapuna Beach. Engineered timber floors, a stone-bench kitchen and full-height glazing create an effortlessly bright interior. The upstairs bedrooms are well separated, and the master opens to a sunny balcony with filtered sea glimpses. Being near-new, it's still under warranty and genuinely low-maintenance, which the owner is happy to put in writing.

title: Compact Furnished Studio in Central Takapuna
description: Smartly designed to make every metre count, this furnished studio offers a clever kitchenette, a Euro laundry and a tiled bathroom. Double glazing keeps it quiet despite the central location, and you can leave the car at home with the beach, supermarket and ferry-bus links all within walking distance. Singles only given the size, and the manager is on-site weekdays if anything ever needs sorting.

title: Charming Seaside Home in Historic Devonport
description: This beautifully presented wooden home blends villa character with relaxed seaside living, just a gentle stroll from Cheltenham Beach and the village ferry. Warm interiors open to a sheltered, established garden that's fully fenced and ideal for kids. Climb nearby Mount Victoria for sweeping harbour views, then catch the ferry into the city in twelve minutes. The owner is overseas for a couple of years, so they're looking for tidy tenants who'll treat it as their own.

title: Warm Four-Bedroom Family Home in Handy Howick
description: Set on a generous, flat section in a family-friendly cul-de-sac, this brick home offers separate living areas, a renovated kitchen and a sprawling fenced backyard for the trampoline and veggie patch. Ducted ventilation and a heat pump make it healthy and warm through winter. Stockade Hill, Howick Village and the beach reserve are all close by, with good schools in zone. The flat lawn and second living room are the quiet heroes here when the weather turns and the kids need somewhere to burn off energy.

title: Sunny double room in a relaxed Ponsonby villa, walk to Three Lamps
description: Spacious double room in a character bungalow tucked just off Jervois Road, sharing with three easygoing professionals. The sun-soaked lounge opens onto a sheltered deck that catches the afternoon light, and the cafes and bars of Ponsonby Road are a five-minute stroll away. A heat pump keeps the place toasty through winter and there's secure off-street parking out front.

title: Cosy single room in leafy Mount Eden, handy to the village
description: A warm single room in a tidy weatherboard home on a quiet tree-lined street near Mount Eden village. Your housemates are two friendly postgrad students who keep things calm and clean during the week. The kitchen was recently redone and double glazing makes the bedrooms wonderfully quiet at night. Maungawhau and its walking tracks are right on the doorstep for weekend wanders.

title: Bright room for rent in a friendly Grey Lynn flat near Williamson Ave
description: Light-filled double room in a renovated villa shared with a sociable group who love a Sunday roast and the odd board-game night. The home has lovely native plantings out back and a sunny deck perfect for summer evenings. You're moments from the Grey Lynn Farmers Market and an easy bus ride into the city. HRV ventilation keeps the whole place fresh and dry year-round.

title: Self-contained studio room in upmarket Remuera, females only
description: A private studio-style room with its own ensuite in a substantial Remuera home, ideal for a working professional after quiet and independence. The wider house is kept immaculate and the leafy street feels a world away from the city, yet Newmarket's shops are minutes down the hill. A walk-in wardrobe and ample storage make settling in effortless. Suits a tidy non-smoker.

title: Double room in a modern Takapuna townhouse, short walk to the beach
description: Generous double room in a near-new townhouse a few hundred metres from Takapuna Beach and Hurstmere Road's restaurants. Two laid-back flatmates who both work locally share the spotless open-plan living area and single garage. The townhouse stays warm and dry thanks to its double glazing, and there's a sunny deck for morning coffee before a beach walk. A great spot for someone wanting the North Shore lifestyle.

title: Room available in a character Devonport bungalow with sea glimpses
description: Charming room in a classic Devonport bungalow filled with original features and afternoon sun. You'll share with a couple of creative types who enjoy the village's slower pace, the weekend markets and the ferry commute across the harbour. Sea glimpses from the front rooms and a fully fenced garden make this a genuinely special place to live. North Head and the beaches are an easy stroll away.

title: Affordable room for a guy in a tidy Howick family home
description: A comfortable single room in a well-kept Howick home, perfect for a student or young tradie after good value out east. Your friendly hosts keep a quiet, respectful household and there's a fully fenced backyard and off-street parking for two cars. Howick village, the bus routes and Botany shops are all close by. The room comes with a heat pump and a generous wardrobe.

title: Budget-friendly room in a spacious Papakura home, meals included
description: Roomy double in a large, sunny Papakura property with plenty of communal space and a big fully fenced section. This is a managed flatshare where weeknight dinners are provided, making it easy for a busy worker to settle in. Motorway access and the train station are both nearby for an easy commute north or south. A single garage and a modern kitchen round out a genuinely good-value option.

title: Sun-Soaked Family Villa with Harbour Glimpses
description: Restored with a careful hand, this character villa keeps its original timber floors and lofty ceilings while opening up into a renovated kitchen and living zone that simply works for family life. North-facing decks soak up the sun from breakfast onward and tease glimpses of the harbour through the trees. On a leafy Mount Victoria street, you can wander to a café, pick up the town belt tracks, or be in the CBD inside ten minutes.

title: Stylish City-Edge Apartment in the Heart of Te Aro
description: Wake up in the buzz of Te Aro, where this light-filled apartment offers easy, lock-up-and-leave living just metres from Cuba Street's bars and boutiques. Floor-to-ceiling windows pour sun across a sleek galley kitchen and a generous living space. A secure car park and double glazing make inner-city life both quiet and effortless.

title: Low-Maintenance Townhouse Moments from Te Aro Cafes
description: Spread over three thoughtfully arranged levels, this near-new townhouse chases the sun from room to room and tucks a private courtyard out the back for weekend get-togethers. The finishes are sharp, and a heat pump plus full double glazing keep things warm and hushed whatever the weather. From the front door it's a flat stroll to the waterfront, the weekend markets and the pulse of the inner city.

title: Elevated Kelburn Home with Sweeping City Views
description: Perched high above the city, this solid family home enjoys jaw-dropping views stretching from the harbour to the hills. Multiple living areas flow out to sun-drenched decks, while the leafy section offers privacy and room for the kids to roam. Walk to the university, the cable car and Kelburn Village in minutes.

title: Classic Karori Family Home on a Generous Section
description: Set back on a sunny, fully fenced section, this much-loved weatherboard home offers space to grow with three double bedrooms and a separate rumpus. The flat lawn and established garden are a rare find, perfect for trampolines and vegetable beds alike. Karori's schools, shops and bush reserves are all close by.

title: Warm, Modern Townhouse in Family-Friendly Karori
description: There's a quiet comfort to this contemporary townhouse, where double glazing and a heat pump take the chill out of every season and keep the open living space easy to heat. The bedrooms are genuinely generous, and the master comes with its own ensuite and walk-in robe. An easy-care courtyard out back means your weekends are spent on Karori's trails and parks, not the lawnmower.

title: Charming Beachside Bungalow a Stroll from Island Bay
description: Just back from the sand, this sweet 1930s bungalow blends original character with a tastefully updated kitchen and bathroom. Sunlight floods the living room, which opens to a sheltered deck made for lazy summer afternoons. The Island Bay village, the parade and the south coast walkway are all an easy wander away.

title: Renovated Coastal Villa with Sea Views in Island Bay
description: A graceful double-bay villa that's been modernised with real respect for its bones, keeping the soaring ceilings and leadlight windows that give it character. The north-facing living wing pulls in sea views and afternoon sun before spilling out to a private deck and landscaped garden. Life in Island Bay looks like morning swims, a flat white at the village and a genuine seaside community.

title: Sunny Petone Character Home Close to the Beach
description: Tucked into one of Petone's friendliest streets, this tidy three-bedroom home offers warm, light-filled living and a flat backyard the whole family can enjoy. The kitchen has been freshly updated and the home stays toasty with a heat pump and good insulation. Jackson Street's eateries and the foreshore are a short, flat walk away.

title: Brand-New Petone Townhouse with Designer Finishes
description: Brand new and built to be kind on the power bill, this architecturally designed townhouse pairs crisp, considered interiors with a private deck that bathes in sun. Stone benchtops and quality appliances anchor the open kitchen, and with double glazing and a heat pump on board, comfort is never a question. The beach, the railway station and buzzing Jackson Street all sit within easy reach.

title: Spacious Lower Hutt Family Home with Double Garage
description: There's room for everyone in this generous brick-and-tile home, with four bedrooms, two living areas and a sunny conservatory overlooking the garden. A full double garage and off-street parking handle the cars, boat and trailer with ease. Quiet, central and handy to schools, parks and the Hutt River trail.

title: Tidy Lower Hutt Unit Perfect for First Home Buyers
description: Affordable and easy-care, this freshly painted two-bedroom unit is an ideal step onto the property ladder or a smart addition to the portfolio. A sunny lounge opens to a small private courtyard, and the single garage adds handy storage. Walk to the bus, the train and Queensgate's shops and cinemas.

title: Premium Te Aro Apartment with Panoramic City Outlook
description: Occupying a sought-after corner of the building, this immaculate apartment frames sweeping city and hill views through wraparound windows. The open living space flows to a sheltered balcony, while the kitchen impresses with stone benchtops and integrated appliances. Secure parking and a lift make this the ultimate low-fuss city base.

title: Sun-Drenched Three-Bedroom Townhouse Steps From Cuba Street
description: This bright modern townhouse sits in the heart of Te Aro, with open-plan living that spills onto a private courtyard ideal for weekend brunches. You're a two-minute stroll from the buzz of Cuba Street's cafes, galleries and night markets, yet the bedrooms stay quiet and warm thanks to double glazing throughout. A heat pump and a sleek galley kitchen with dishwasher round out a genuinely low-maintenance city lifestyle.

title: Furnished Top-Floor Apartment With Harbour Glimpses in Te Aro
description: Perched on the top floor of a well-run inner-city block, this fully furnished one-bedroom apartment catches afternoon sun and a slice of harbour beyond the rooftops. Everything is ready to go for a professional or couple wanting to walk to the waterfront, Te Papa and the CBD without ever touching a car. Secure entry, a tidy modern kitchen and a heat pump make it an easy, lock-up-and-leave home.

title: Charming Character Villa on the Slopes of Mount Victoria
description: A classic Wellington villa with high ceilings, native timber floors and the kind of character that's hard to find this close to town. Tucked into a leafy Mount Victoria street, it offers four double bedrooms and a sun-trap front room perfect for lazy Sunday mornings. The town belt walking tracks start almost at the door, and Courtenay Place is a downhill ten-minute wander away.

title: Quiet Two-Bedroom Apartment Near the Mount Vic Town Belt
description: Set back from the road in a small, friendly complex, this two-bedroom apartment is a warm and easy base for anyone wanting greenery on the doorstep. Both bedrooms are doubles, the lounge opens to a sheltered balcony, and double glazing keeps things peaceful and snug through the southerly. Bus routes into the city are a short walk away, with the town belt trails just beyond.

title: Elevated Family Home With Harbour Views in Kelburn
description: Wake up to wide views across the harbour from this elevated Kelburn home, a short stroll from the cable car and Victoria University. Three generous bedrooms, a sunny north-facing deck and a fully fenced backyard make it a standout for families or a sharing household. The botanic gardens are practically next door, and you can be on campus or in the CBD within minutes.

title: Cosy Furnished Studio Walk to Victoria University, Kelburn
description: A neat, fully furnished studio that's perfect for a student or solo professional who wants to live a flat walk from Vic Uni. The clever layout packs in a kitchenette, study nook and a surprisingly roomy sleeping area, all kept warm by a heat pump and insulation. Step outside and you're moments from Kelburn village's cafes and the bus straight down to town.

title: Spacious Four-Bedroom Family Home in Leafy Karori
description: Plenty of room to spread out in this well-kept Karori home, with four bedrooms, two living areas and a flat fenced lawn the kids and dog will love. The double garage and ample off-street parking are a rare bonus, and zoning for sought-after local schools makes it a smart family move. Karori's shops, pools and Zealandia sanctuary are all just minutes from the door.

title: Warm Modern Townhouse With Double Glazing in Karori
description: Built to a high standard, this low-maintenance townhouse stays toasty year-round with double glazing, HRV ventilation and a heat pump downstairs. Three bedrooms sit upstairs away from the open-plan living, which opens to a private courtyard catching the afternoon sun. It's an easy walk to Karori Mall and the express bus, making the city commute a breeze.

title: Beachside Bungalow a Short Stroll From Island Bay Surf
description: Salt air and the sound of the waves come standard at this character bungalow, just a few minutes from the Island Bay shoreline and The Parade's cafes. Two double bedrooms, a sunny living room and a fully fenced garden make it a relaxed coastal retreat for a couple or small family. Bring the bikes for the waterfront path, or chase the south coast sunsets year-round.

title: Tidy Sunny Two-Bedroom Unit Near Island Bay Village
description: An easy-care two-bedroom unit in a quiet Island Bay cul-de-sac, freshly painted and ready to move into. It catches all-day sun, has its own patch of fenced garden, and sits within walking distance of the village shops and the beach. A heat pump and a single carport make winter living simple, perfect for downsizers or first-home renters.

title: Stylish Townhouse Moments From the Petone Foreshore
description: Live the best of Petone in this contemporary three-bedroom townhouse, a short walk to both Jackson Street's eateries and the foreshore boardwalk. Sun pours into the open-plan living, which flows to a low-maintenance deck made for summer evenings. With an internal-access single garage, double glazing and a master ensuite, it's an effortless, modern home in a genuinely vibrant suburb.

title: Renovated Three-Bedroom Home in Central Lower Hutt
description: Recently refreshed throughout, this comfortable three-bedroom home offers easy single-level living close to Westfield Queensgate and the Hutt River trail. The modern kitchen opens to a sunny dining space, while a heat pump and fresh insulation keep the whole house warm and dry. A fully fenced section and off-street parking for two make it a practical pick for a growing family.

title: Sunny double room in laid-back Te Aro flat, walk to everything
description: A warm double room has opened up in a tidy four-bedroom flat tucked just off Cuba Street, where cafes, gigs and the night market are all on your doorstep. The room catches the afternoon sun and shares a recently redone kitchen and a cosy lounge that the current flatmates actually use. A heat pump keeps things toasty through winter, and you are a five-minute stroll from the waterfront when the weather plays nice.

title: Room available in friendly Te Aro apartment-share, quiet professionals
description: Looking for someone easygoing to take the spare room in our two-flatmate setup on the edge of Te Aro. It is a calm, clean household that suits people who work hard during the week and enjoy a slow Sunday. The building has secure entry and double glazing that keeps the city noise out, and you can be at the train station or Lambton Quay inside ten minutes on foot.

title: Private studio room with sea glimpses in character Mount Victoria villa
description: Tucked into a classic Mount Victoria villa, this self-contained studio room gives you your own space while still being part of a relaxed share house. High ceilings and original sash windows give it real character, and on a clear day you can catch glimpses of the harbour over the rooftops. The town belt walking tracks start almost at the gate, and the cafes of Majoribanks Street are a short downhill wander away.

title: Bright girls-only room in leafy Mount Victoria share house
description: A spacious room is going in a welcoming, girls-only household on a quiet Mount Victoria street lined with old pohutukawa. The flat is sociable without being chaotic, with a big shared kitchen where Sunday baking tends to happen. You are walking distance to the Basin Reserve and a quick bus into the city, and the green of the town belt is right behind the house for weekend runs.

title: Cosy room in warm Kelburn flat, minutes from Vic Uni
description: This double room suits a student or young professional who wants to live a short walk from Victoria University and the cable car. The flat sits high enough to grab views down the gully, and the current crew are a mellow mix who share cooking now and then. A proper heat pump and ceiling insulation make it one of the warmer Kelburn flats, which anyone who has wintered up here will appreciate.

title: Large boys-only room in spacious Karori family home
description: Plenty of room to spread out in this big Karori house, where a generous double bedroom is up for grabs in an all-male flat of three. The place has a proper backyard, off-street parking and a garage for bikes or gear, which is rare this close to town. Karori village shops, the supermarket and the bus into the CBD are all an easy walk, and Zealandia is on your doorstep for the weekends.

title: Beachy room in relaxed Island Bay flat, surf on your doorstep
description: Wake up to the salt air in this easygoing Island Bay flat just a couple of streets back from the beach. The room is light and airy, opening onto a sunny deck that gets the morning sun and is made for slow coffees. Flatmates here are into the outdoors, with the south coast walks and Tapu Te Ranga marae trails close by, and the number 1 bus rolls straight into the city.

title: Warm renovated room in Petone flat, walk to Jackson Street
description: A freshly painted room is available in a recently renovated Petone flat, an easy stroll from the cafes, breweries and vintage shops of Jackson Street. The house is double glazed and properly insulated, so it stays dry and warm through the cold months, and there is off-street parking out front. Flatmates are a friendly, low-key pair, and the train into Wellington takes barely fifteen minutes from the nearby station.

title: Renovated Brick Bungalow Moments from Riccarton Mall
description: This 1950s brick-and-tile bungalow has been brought right up to date without losing the solid character that makes these homes so easy to live in. The old galley has gone, replaced by a generous open kitchen that anchors a reconfigured living wing; sliders lead out to a north-facing deck and a securely fenced lawn where the kids and the dog can run. You're a few minutes from Bush Inn and Westfield, with the University, Hagley Park and an express bus into town all within reach. The street itself is the kind that stays quiet, lined with established trees.

title: Near-New Low-Maintenance Townhouse in the Heart of Riccarton
description: If you want the location without the upkeep, this is worth a look. Three double bedrooms sit over two tidy levels, with a stone benchtop in the kitchen and gas-instant hot water that never runs cold. The double glazing keeps road noise out and warmth in, and the small rear courtyard is exactly enough garden to enjoy and not enough to become a chore. Cafes, supermarkets and the hospital corridor are all an easy walk along Riccarton Road, which makes it as sensible a rental as it is a first home in a pocket that rarely comes up.

title: Gracious Character Home on a Leafy Fendalton Street
description: Behind a clipped hedge on one of Fendalton's address streets sits a home that has aged with real grace. High ceilings, leadlight windows and original joinery give the formal rooms their presence, while the family kitchen has been opened up for the way people actually cook and gather now. Out back, decades-old plantings shelter a lawn that was made for long lunches that drift into evening. Add the school zones buyers chase and you have something that doesn't trade hands often, and rewards those who recognise it.

title: Sun-Filled Family Home in Prized Fendalton School Zones
description: Families tend to fall for this one quickly, and it's easy to see why. The main living area catches sun for most of the day and opens straight onto a paved patio and lawn, while a separate rumpus gives the kids somewhere of their own and a study nook handles the working-from-home days. Ducted heating and double glazing mean it stays comfortable through a Canterbury winter without a thought. The schools and parks are close enough to walk to, and Merivale's shops and eateries are only a few minutes by car.

title: Classic Merivale Home with Established Gardens and Off-Street Parking
description: A weatherboard classic in blue-chip Merivale, where the original character has been kept honest and the kitchen and bathroom quietly modernised to match. The section is the real surprise: north-facing, framed by mature trees, with a sheltered deck that holds the sun from your morning coffee through to a glass of something at the end of the day. The village and its well-known cafes and delis are a short walk, and you can be in the city or cutting across Hagley Park on the bike in no time.

title: Stylish Lock-Up-and-Leave Apartment in Vibrant Merivale
description: Tucked into a boutique low-rise just off Papanui Road, this two-bedroom apartment is the easy, secure base a lot of buyers are after. The galley kitchen is sleek and well thought out, and the private balcony pulls in good afternoon sun. A heat pump and double glazing keep things snug year-round, and with a single carpark and lift access, coming and going never involves stairs or hunting for a space. Step outside and Merivale's dining, shopping and gyms are right there.

title: Coastal Sumner Home with Sweeping Sea Views
description: Set up high in Sumner, this home makes the most of its position with wide windows that look clean across the bay to the horizon. Living areas open onto a sheltered deck that's perfect for a sundowner once the day's surfers head in, and the upstairs master keeps that same outlook to itself along with its own ensuite. The beach, the village cafes and Cave Rock are a short walk down the hill, so the coastal life everyone talks about is genuinely on your doorstep here, not a drive away.

title: Relaxed Beachside Townhouse a Short Stroll to Sumner Village
description: Just back from the Esplanade, this two-storey townhouse trades a big section for the kind of low-maintenance living that leaves time for the beach. Downstairs the living spaces are bright and open onto a private courtyard; upstairs the bedrooms catch the sea breeze and the afternoon sun. The best part is that the car can largely stay parked, with the sand, the weekend markets and Sumner's famous fish-and-chips all an easy wander away. It's a tidy, salt-air lifestyle without the work that usually comes with it.

title: Smart Brick Unit in Handy, Up-and-Coming Addington
description: For a first home or a straightforward rental, this two-bedroom brick unit ticks the boxes. It has been freshly painted and recarpeted, so there's nothing to do but move in, and the lounge gets good sun off a kitchen that's compact but does everything it needs to. The yard is fully fenced, with room for a vege patch or somewhere for the dog, and there's off-street parking right at the door. Tower Junction, the stadium precinct and a short hop into the central city are all close by.

title: Architecturally Designed Townhouse in Buzzing Addington
description: There's nothing off-the-shelf about this one. A clever split-level layout, engineered oak underfoot and a dramatic black kitchen give it real personality, and a sun-trap upper deck is where you'll end up most evenings. Behind the looks it's been built properly, warm and dry with full double glazing and an HRV system doing the quiet work. Addington's breweries, restaurants and the raceway are all walkable, and the cycleways will have you in the central city without ever touching the car.

title: Spacious Modern Family Home on a Generous Rolleston Section
description: Space is the headline here. Four bedrooms and a vast open-plan living, dining and kitchen hub give a growing family room to spread out, with a separate media room for the nights that call for a movie and the door shut. The living opens onto a covered alfresco area and a fully fenced lawn, the master has its walk-in robe and ensuite, and ducted heating keeps the whole place even through winter. It sits on a sunny, easy-care section close to schools and the Rolleston town centre, in a part of Canterbury that keeps drawing people for good reason.

title: Sunny North-Facing Section Ready to Build in Rolleston
description: Here's your blank canvas in one of Canterbury's fastest-growing towns. The section is level and north-facing in a tidy new subdivision, services are run to the boundary, and the title is already issued, so there's no waiting around to bring in a builder and get started. Rolleston has earned its popularity with families, who come for the schools, the sports fields and the quick motorway link that puts Christchurch and the airport within easy reach. Sections like this don't sit for long.

title: Charming Country-Edge Home in Welcoming Rangiora
description: On the leafy fringe of Rangiora, this is the sort of place that asks you to slow down a little. Wide verandahs wrap around a character home, a rambling cottage garden surrounds it, and inside a modern kitchen and a wood-burner keep family life warm and unhurried. There's genuine room for the kids and a separate workshop for whoever in the household likes a project. The town centre, the schools and the rail trail are all a short drive, close enough to be handy but far enough to feel like proper country.

title: Sun-soaked three-bedroom family home near Riccarton shops
description: Tucked away on a quiet leafy street, this weatherboard home catches all-day sun across its open-plan living and kitchen. You're a short stroll from Riccarton Mall, the bustling night market and excellent zoned schools, with the bus exchange minutes away. A heat pump and double glazing keep things cosy through the Canterbury winter, while the fully fenced back lawn is ideal for kids or a dog.

title: Modern furnished two-bedroom apartment in central Riccarton
description: Bright and genuinely low-maintenance, this first-floor apartment arrives fully furnished, so you can simply unpack and settle in. Tall windows pour light into the living space, and the secure complex delivers the kind of lock-and-leave ease that suits busy weeks. Cafes, the University of Canterbury bus route and Westfield all sit within an easy walk. HRV ventilation paired with a heat pump keeps it comfortable for a couple or working professional whatever the season.

title: Elegant four-bedroom character home in leafy Fendalton
description: Set behind established hedging on one of Fendalton's most sought-after avenues, this gracious home blends period charm with thoughtful modern updates. High ceilings, native timber floors and a sun-drenched north-facing living room create a warm, refined feel. The private landscaped garden is framed by mature natives, and you're zoned for some of Christchurch's top schools. A standout family residence in a blue-ribbon pocket.

title: Stylish three-bedroom townhouse moments from Fendalton Village
description: Near-new and built for easy living, this townhouse delivers space without the upkeep of a larger property. The ground floor flows from a sleek kitchen out to a sheltered, sunny courtyard, and three roomy bedrooms wait upstairs, the master complete with a walk-in wardrobe. Double glazing and full insulation help keep the power bills sensible. With Fendalton Village, Hagley Park and the central city all within reach, it suits a family chasing a premium address with minimal fuss.

title: Boutique one-bedroom apartment in vibrant Merivale
description: Live right on Merivale's cafe and boutique strip in this smart, light-filled one-bedroom apartment. The open living area spills onto a sheltered balcony made for slow morning coffees, and the kitchen comes fitted with quality appliances. Secure off-street parking and a quick walk to the city make it an easy base for a professional, yet it stays surprisingly peaceful once the door closes.

title: Spacious four-bedroom home with double garage in Merivale
description: A substantial brick-and-tile home offering room for the whole family across two generous living areas. The renovated kitchen overlooks a private, fully fenced backyard with an established lawn and a sunny entertaining deck. You're walking distance to Merivale Mall, leafy parks and well-regarded schools. With a heat pump, double glazing and ample off-street parking, this one ticks every box.

title: Beachside three-bedroom retreat with sea views in Sumner
description: Wake to the sound of the waves in this relaxed coastal home perched above Sumner village. The upstairs living opens to a deck capturing sweeping views out to the estuary and beach, where you can watch the surf roll in. Stroll down to the cafes, the historic Cave Rock and the swimming beach in minutes. A genuine seaside lifestyle with a heat pump and sunny aspect to match.

title: Cosy furnished studio steps from Sumner beach
description: A charming, fully self-contained studio sitting just a two-minute walk from the sand. Every metre has been put to work, and it comes furnished with a compact kitchenette and a sunny little living nook. It is a natural fit for a single professional or student who wants the village cafe scene and a morning swim on the doorstep. Off-street parking and a heat pump finish off this tidy beachside gem.

title: Warm two-bedroom townhouse in handy Addington
description: Positioned for genuine inner-city convenience, this tidy two-bedroom townhouse sits a short hop from the Addington cafes, the stadium and the South City precinct. The open-plan ground floor is light and easy to care for, opening to a private courtyard that comes into its own for summer barbecues. A heat pump and double glazing keep it snug through winter, and there's a single garage plus extra off-street parking. Strong value in a pocket that keeps growing.

title: Neat two-bedroom unit close to Addington amenities
description: A well-kept brick unit that puts comfortable, affordable living within easy reach of the city. The living room opens to a small private garden, and the kitchen has just been refreshed with modern fittings. Between the heat pump, the insulation and dedicated off-street parking, it makes for an uncomplicated choice for a couple or small family. Buses and the Hagley Park cycleways are only around the corner.

title: Brand-new four-bedroom family home in growing Rolleston
description: Be the first to live in this immaculate new build, tucked into one of Rolleston's freshest subdivisions. Expansive open-plan living connects straight through to a covered alfresco area and a fully fenced lawn, so indoor and outdoor space work together for family life. A double garage, walk-in wardrobe and ensuite to the master lift the quality, while full insulation and double glazing make it warm and efficient. Schools, parks and the town centre all sit just minutes from the door.

title: Charming three-bedroom home on a quiet Rangiora street
description: Enjoy the relaxed pace of Rangiora township in this comfortable three-bedroom home on a generous, established section. The sunny lounge opens onto a sheltered deck overlooking a fully fenced garden with room for the kids and a vege patch. A wood burner and heat pump keep it toasty in winter, and the schools, pool and town centre are an easy walk. Rural-edge living within commuting distance of the city.

title: Sunny double room in a warm Riccarton flat, walk to the mall and uni
description: A spacious double room has just opened up in a tidy, well-heated weatherboard flat a couple of minutes from Westfield Riccarton and an easy cycle to the University of Canterbury. The lounge spills out onto a north-facing deck that holds the afternoon sun, and the shared kitchen got a refresh last winter with a new oven and dishwasher. Buses into town run constantly from the corner, so you can skip the car most days. We're a relaxed bunch who keep the common areas clean but aren't fussy about much else.

title: Quiet room for a tidy flatmate in leafy Riccarton, off-street parking
description: We're two long-term residents after one easy-going flatmate to fill the spare room in our settled Riccarton home, tucked behind a row of mature oaks. It's carpeted and double-glazed, and a heat pump we put in last year keeps it cosy right through the frosts. The fully fenced back garden is made for summer barbecues, and both the local shops and Hagley Park are a short stroll away. We're quiet during the week and happy to share the odd meal, but otherwise give each other plenty of space.

title: Bright room with ensuite in a modern Fendalton townhouse
description: This is a premium room with its own ensuite and a walk-in wardrobe, set in a near-new townhouse on one of Fendalton's quiet, tree-lined streets. The whole place is insulated and double-glazed, with HRV ventilation that keeps it fresh and dry even on the dampest Canterbury mornings. You're in the heart of the city's best school zones and a ten-minute drive from the central city. It suits a professional who wants a calm, low-maintenance home to come back to at the end of the day.

title: Furnished room in a friendly girls' flat near Merivale village
description: Three of us are looking for a fourth to join our character bungalow, just around the corner from the boutiques and brunch spots of Merivale village. The room is ready to move straight into with a queen bed and built-in storage, and the house was reinsulated and fitted with a heat pump a couple of winters back, so there's no shivering through July. Out the back there's a sunny native garden, and the bus into town stops at the end of the street. We're a sociable, drama-free flat and happy to show new flatmates the ropes.

title: Self-contained studio with sea views in beachside Sumner
description: Perched on the Sumner hillside with a clear outlook across the bay, this self-contained studio lets you wake up to the sound of the surf. It has its own kitchenette and bathroom, a heat pump for the cooler months, and a small private deck that's perfect for a morning coffee while the tide turns. Trade the hum of the city for the village's easy pace, with the beach a few minutes' walk downhill and the Scarborough cliffs right on your doorstep. It's a self-contained spot, so you come and go entirely on your own terms.

title: Affordable room in a relaxed Addington flat close to the city
description: Good-value room going in a laid-back, sociable flat right in the thick of Addington, with the breweries, the bars on Lincoln Road and the showgrounds all walkable. A heat pump and decent insulation keep the place warm, and there's off-street parking plus a bus stop more or less outside the gate for the quick run into the central city. It's a solid pick if you want to be central without paying central-city rent, and the current flatmates are easy company when you feel like it.

title: Spacious room in a modern Rolleston family home, fully fenced
description: There's a generous room with a walk-in wardrobe going in a near-new home in one of Rolleston's quieter newer subdivisions. The house is fully insulated and double-glazed, with a heat pump that handles the cold mornings out this way, and it opens onto a fully fenced section with a deck that bakes in the long Canterbury summers. The town centre, supermarket and the motorway on-ramp are all only a few minutes down the road, which makes the commute or the weekend run into the city painless. A settled, comfortable base for someone after a bit of breathing room.

title: Warm room in a settled Rangiora flat with a big garden
description: One quiet, considerate flatmate wanted for a settled home on the leafy edge of Rangiora, a short walk from the town centre and the regular commuter bus into Christchurch. The double room is carpeted and properly warm, with a heat pump and HRV ventilation taking care of the winter damp. The real draw is the section: a big, fully fenced plot with established fruit trees and more than enough room to put in a vege patch if that's your thing. It suits someone who values a slower pace and doesn't mind being a touch out of town.

title: Sun-Drenched Family Home in the Heart of Hamilton East
description: Tucked behind a mature hedge on a leafy street, this character weatherboard home blends original villa charm with a thoughtfully extended open-plan living wing. Walk to the cafes and riverside trails that make Hamilton East one of the city's most sought-after pockets, with the University and Waikato Hospital just minutes away. The north-facing deck spills onto a fully fenced lawn that's ideal for kids and dogs alike.

title: Low-Maintenance Brick Townhouse Steps from Chartwell Shops
description: Designed for easy living, this near-new brick-and-cedar townhouse offers a crisp, light-filled interior across two levels. The location is unbeatable for convenience, with Chartwell Square, supermarkets and bus links all within a short stroll. Lock up and leave for the weekend knowing your courtyard garden needs nothing more than the occasional trim.

title: Modern Four-Bedroom Living in Popular Rototuna
description: This contemporary plaster-clad home was built for growing families who want space without compromise. A generous open kitchen with a butler's pantry flows to a covered alfresco area perfect for summer barbecues. Set within the catchment of well-regarded Rototuna schools, you're also a quick drive from the Rototuna Town Centre, North City shops and the surrounding reserve walkways.

title: Elevated Hillcrest Home with Leafy Outlook
description: Perched to capture all-day sun, this solid 1970s home rewards with treetop views and a peaceful, established setting. The original layout has been freshened with new carpet and a re-clad kitchen, leaving room to add your own touches over time. Handy to the University, Hillcrest High and the Waikato River paths, it's a smart entry into a tightly held street.

title: Character Bungalow in Sought-After Cambridge
description: Brimming with personality, this restored 1930s bungalow pairs polished native timber floors with a sympathetic modern kitchen and bathroom. Stroll to Cambridge's tree-lined main street, boutique cafes and the Avantidrome within minutes. The deep section offers established fruit trees, a vegetable garden and plenty of room for the trampoline.

title: Stylish Riverside Apartment in Hamilton East
description: Enjoy a top-floor outlook over the Waikato River from this stylish two-bedroom apartment. Floor-to-ceiling glass frames the water and floods the open living space with light, while a secure basement carpark takes the guesswork out of city parking. Cross the bridge to the CBD or wander to Hamilton East's vibrant eateries in moments.

title: Contemporary Three-Bed Townhouse in Rototuna
description: Freshly completed and ready to move in, this double-storey townhouse delivers crisp modern lines and a smart, efficient floorplan. Downstairs opens to a private patio and easy-care lawn, while three robe-fitted bedrooms upstairs include a serene master with ensuite. A short hop from Rototuna's town centre, schools and sports fields.

title: Spacious Family Entertainer in Chartwell
description: Set on a generous flat section, this much-loved brick home has been extended to create a wonderful indoor-outdoor flow around a sunny private courtyard. Multiple living areas suit a busy household, and the separate rumpus could easily become a teen retreat or home office. Zoned for popular schools and an easy walk to Chartwell's shopping and reserves.

title: Warm and Tidy Starter Home in Hillcrest
description: Ideal for first-home buyers or investors, this neat brick-and-tile home is warmer than most thanks to recent insulation and a modern heat pump. The fully fenced backyard is a safe haven for little ones, and the single garage offers handy storage or workshop space. Close to the University, bus routes and local shops for everyday ease.

title: Executive Living on a Quiet Cambridge Cul-de-Sac
description: Quality craftsmanship is evident throughout this architecturally designed home, where a soaring central living space anchors the floorplan. A sleek scullery, gas fire and luxurious tiled ensuite elevate the everyday, while the landscaped grounds offer privacy and a tranquil backdrop. Minutes to Cambridge village yet wrapped in rural calm.

title: Easy-Care Brick Unit Near Chartwell Square
description: Perfectly positioned for downsizers and savvy investors, this sunny single-level unit is part of a well-kept block of just four. Open-plan living connects to a private, low-maintenance patio that catches the afternoon sun. With shops, transport and reserves all close by, it's an effortless lifestyle in a popular Hamilton suburb.

title: Renovated Riverside Charmer in Hamilton East
description: Lovingly modernised from top to toe, this 1950s home now boasts a designer kitchen, reconfigured bathrooms and seamless flow to a sheltered entertaining deck. The leafy street sits within easy reach of the river loop, Hamilton East shops and excellent schooling. Move straight in and enjoy the work that's already been done.

title: Smart Two-Storey Townhouse in Growing Rototuna
description: A clever choice for professionals and small families, this tidy townhouse offers durable finishes and a sun-filled upstairs lounge. The fenced rear yard is a rare bonus at this price, giving pets and gardeners room to play. With the northern arterials and Rototuna's shops close at hand, the daily commute and the weekend grocery run are both a breeze.

title: Sun-Drenched Three-Bedroom Family Home in Leafy Hamilton East
description: Tucked away on a quiet street within strolling distance of the river path, this weatherboard charmer pairs original character with a freshly renovated kitchen. The north-facing living room flows onto a sunny deck that catches the afternoon light, while the fully fenced backyard gives kids and pets room to roam. Walk to the cafes of Cook Street or cycle to Waikato University in minutes.

title: Stylish Low-Maintenance Townhouse Steps from The Base, Chartwell
description: Never been lived in, this two-storey townhouse is the kind of place you settle into without a to-do list. The heat pump and double glazing mean winter mornings stay mild, and the ground-floor living spills out to a sheltered courtyard that's just right for a flat white before work. Three good bedrooms sit upstairs, each with built-in robes. With Chartwell Shopping Centre around the corner and the northbound motorway minutes away, it's an effortless base for professionals or a small family.

title: Spacious Four-Bedroom Home with Double Garage in Rototuna
description: Designed for growing families, this generous brick-and-tile home offers two living areas, a separate study nook and a kitchen that opens straight to the entertainer's patio. The master suite tucks away at the rear with its own ensuite and walk-in wardrobe. Zoned for sought-after Rototuna schools and a short drive to the town centre, it ticks every box for settled family life.

title: Furnished One-Bedroom Apartment by the Waikato River, Hamilton East
description: Perfectly positioned for riverside walks, this fully furnished apartment is move-in ready with everything from the bed linen to the toaster sorted. Floor-to-ceiling windows draw in plenty of light and frame leafy outlooks across the suburb. Ideal for a student or visiting professional who wants the buzz of nearby cafes without the upkeep of a section.

title: Charming Renovated Cottage on a Quiet Cambridge Street
description: Full of character and freshly painted throughout, this two-bedroom cottage blends timber floors and high ceilings with a crisp new bathroom. The established native garden hums with tui and creates a private retreat behind the picket fence. A gentle stroll from the leafy town centre and Lake Te Ko Utu, it offers the relaxed Cambridge village lifestyle so many are after.

title: Modern Two-Bedroom Townhouse Near Hillcrest Shops
description: Bright and contemporary, this tidy townhouse makes the most of every square metre with an open-plan living zone spilling out to a low-maintenance courtyard. Both bedrooms are doubles, and the HRV system keeps the home fresh and dry through winter. Close to the University, Hillcrest amenities and bus routes into the city, it's a smart choice for flatmates or a couple.

title: Warm Three-Bedroom Home with Big Backyard in Hillcrest
description: There's real room to breathe here: a flat, fully fenced section with mature fruit trees and plenty of lawn for a trampoline, a veggie patch, or the dog to tear around. The lounge has just been recarpeted and warms up fast thanks to the heat pump and an insulation upgrade. Local primary schools are within easy reach and Waikato Hospital is barely five minutes by car, making this a sensible, comfortable rental for a household that wants space without the fuss.

title: Compact Furnished Studio Close to Chartwell Square
description: Neat, warm and fully self-contained, this furnished studio is an easy-care option for a single tenant or student. A clever fit-out makes the most of the space, with a kitchenette, ensuite bathroom and a sunny window seat to unwind in. Walking distance to Chartwell shopping and on a direct bus line into central Hamilton.

title: Executive Four-Bedroom Family Residence in Rototuna
description: Set in one of Rototuna's newer pockets, this near-new home leads with a chef's kitchen and a walk-in scullery that keeps the mess out of sight when you're entertaining. The open-plan living opens onto a landscaped, fully fenced yard, and a second lounge gives the kids somewhere of their own. Tucked behind the master wing is an ensuite and a roomy walk-in robe, while ducted heating and double glazing handle the temperature year-round.

title: Tidy Two-Bedroom Brick Unit in Central Cambridge
description: Low-maintenance and beautifully kept, this brick unit suits downsizers or a couple wanting a lock-and-leave base in the heart of Cambridge. The open lounge stays toasty with a heat pump, and the private rear patio is a sun-trap for weekend brunches. Just a short walk to the Thursday market, boutique shops and tree-lined Victoria Street.

title: Bright Two-Bedroom Apartment Handy to Waikato University, Hillcrest
description: A clever layout and plenty of natural light make this first-floor apartment a comfortable pick for students or young professionals. Both bedrooms fit doubles with ease, and the shared lounge opens to a small balcony with a green outlook. Set on a quiet cul-de-sac yet minutes from the University, Hillcrest shops and the bus into town.

title: Sunny Three-Bedroom Weatherboard Home in Hamilton East
description: This well-loved weatherboard classic wears its age beautifully, with polished timber floors, a proper separate dining room and a wide front porch made for slow summer evenings. A large heat pump and ceiling insulation keep things snug once the temperature drops, and the fenced rear garden is private enough for the dog and quiet enough for a Sunday read. The river, the Saturday markets and the Grey Street cafe strip are all an easy walk from the gate.

title: Sunny double room in friendly Hamilton East flat, walk to Waikato Uni
description: A bright, north-facing double room has just opened up in our relaxed four-person flat tucked into leafy Hamilton East. You're a five-minute stroll from the river walkway and an easy bike ride to the university, with cafes and the Saturday farmers' market right on your doorstep. The flatmates are a mix of postgrads and young professionals who'll happily rope you into the odd Sunday roast but otherwise keep to their own schedules. The heat pump keeps the lounge toasty through winter, and there's off-street parking if you've got a car.

title: Quiet room for a working professional in Chartwell, close to the mall
description: We're after one easygoing flatmate to fill a carpeted single room in our tidy, well-insulated Chartwell home. The location is hard to beat — a couple of minutes from Chartwell Square, the bus into town stops right past the gate, and there's a cracking little dog park around the corner. The two of us are thirty-somethings who work full time, value a clean kitchen, and have long since outgrown loud parties. The room catches lovely afternoon sun and the shared living area has its own heat pump for the colder months.

title: Spacious room in modern Rototuna home with double glazing and deck
description: A generous room with its own walk-in wardrobe is going in this near-new home in the heart of fast-growing Rototuna. The whole house is double glazed and genuinely warm, and the sunny deck off the kitchen sees plenty of weekend barbecue action once summer rolls around. You'll have the Rototuna town centre, supermarkets and good schools all within walking distance, plus a straightforward commute whether you're heading north or into the CBD. Current flatmates are a couple and one other tenant — a calm household that's respectful of shared space.

title: Affordable room in a sociable Hillcrest student flat near campus
description: One room is opening up in a lively but well-kept Hillcrest flat that's pretty much made for students — campus is a short walk away and the bus stop sits metres from the front door. It's a social household that runs flat dinners on Wednesdays, though everyone quietly knuckles down once exam season hits. The room is a comfortable single with built-in storage, and good ventilation keeps the whole place dry through winter. Bills are split evenly and the landlord is quick to sort any maintenance that crops up.

title: Self-contained studio with ensuite in leafy Cambridge, semi-rural feel
description: Tucked at the back of a character property on the Cambridge fringe, this self-contained studio gives you your own ensuite, kitchenette and private entry while still keeping you part of a friendly household. Cambridge is all tree-lined streets, boutique cafes and weekend bike trails, and you're only a short drive from the velodrome and the river. It suits someone chasing a bit of independence without the cost of renting solo. The studio has been freshly insulated and runs its own heat pump, with off-street parking for one car.

title: Girls-only room in a warm, tidy Hamilton East villa near the river
description: There's a welcoming room going in our all-female flat, set in a renovated villa just back from the Waikato River in Hamilton East. The house has been reclad and insulated, so it stays genuinely warm in winter, and the wide front verandah is everyone's favourite spot for a morning coffee. The current flatmates are nurses and teachers who keep things calm, clean and considerate. You're close to the hospital, the river path and no shortage of brunch options, with a single garage available for a small extra weekly charge.

title: Boys' flat in Hillcrest has a room going, easy walk to the uni
description: Three of us in a solid Hillcrest place are on the hunt for a fourth to take a good-sized room with its own desk nook. We're a pretty low-key crew — a couple of engineering students and one tradie — happy to share a beer on a Friday but not big on drama. The house has a fully fenced backyard, a decent heat pump and a carport out front for whoever lands the spot. Handy to campus, the local dairy and the Five Cross Roads shops, with the bus into town only a few minutes away.

title: Bright room in a peaceful Rototuna household, ideal for a quiet flatmate
description: A sunny double room is up for grabs in a settled Rototuna home where the vibe leans relaxed and considerate rather than party-central. The owners are a semi-retired couple who fold meals into the weekly rate, so it's a great fit if you're new to Hamilton or just appreciate a bit of home cooking. The garden is fully fenced with established natives, and the lounge stays cosy thanks to a heat pump and double glazing throughout. You're within walking distance of the parks, the lake reserve and the Rototuna shops.

title: Sun-Drenched Family Home Steps from Mount Main Beach
description: Wake to salt air with the surf at Mount Maunganui's main beach just a short stroll away. This light-filled four-bedroom home blends open-plan living with a north-facing deck made for long summer evenings, and the chef's kitchen flows out to a fully fenced backyard. The master suite catches glimpses of Mauao, while heat pumps and double glazing keep things comfortable year-round. A rare pairing of true beachside lifestyle and everyday family practicality.

title: Low-Maintenance Coastal Townhouse Near the Mount Base Track
description: Just moments from the Mauao base track and the harbourside cafe strip, this stylish three-bedroom townhouse is built for easy living. The private courtyard soaks up the afternoon sun, while double glazing and a heat pump keep the interior cosy through every season. Internal-access garaging and a contemporary kitchen take the effort out of the everyday. It suits professionals, downsizers, or anyone eyeing a smart holiday investment near the water.

title: Top-Floor Apartment with Sweeping Ocean Outlook at the Mount
description: From its top-floor vantage point, this two-bedroom apartment frames an uninterrupted sweep of ocean across the Bay. Floor-to-ceiling glass opens onto a sheltered balcony where the surfers are in view from first light. A sleek galley kitchen, secure parking, and an ensuite off the master round out a thoughtfully compact layout. For anyone chasing the Mount Maunganui lifestyle without the upkeep, this one delivers.

title: Classic Mount Bungalow on a Generous Sunny Section
description: Set on a wide, sun-soaked section in an established Mount Maunganui street, this character three-bedroom bungalow has room to grow. Polished timber floors and high ceilings bring warmth indoors, and the deep backyard leaves plenty of space for kids, pets, or a future extension. Shops, schools, and the beach are all only minutes from the gate. It is a much-loved family home ready to be written into its next chapter.

title: Modern Family Living Close to Papamoa Beach and Schools
description: In one of Papamoa's most sought-after pockets, this near-new four-bedroom home is all about easy indoor-outdoor flow. The open-plan living zone spills onto a north-facing deck and a fully fenced lawn that kids and pets will love, while a separate media room gives the family somewhere of their own. The kitchen pairs a walk-in pantry with stone benchtops for everyday function. Beach, parks, and schools all sit within a comfortable stroll.

title: Brand-New Papamoa Townhouse Within a Quiet Coastal Enclave
description: Freshly completed and never lived in, this three-bedroom townhouse brings crisp contemporary design to a quiet Papamoa cul-de-sac. Full insulation and HRV ventilation make for a warm, healthy home, while the open kitchen and living area step out to a low-care courtyard. The master comes with its own ensuite and walk-in wardrobe for a touch of private retreat. It is a smart way into beachside living with very little to maintain.

title: Spacious Papamoa Home with Pool-Sized Backyard
description: Built for big families and long summer entertaining, this five-bedroom home sprawls across a generous section. Expansive living areas open to a sheltered deck and a lawn with genuine room for a future pool, while a double garage, separate study, and plenty of storage keep daily life organised. Papamoa Plaza and the dune walkways are close at hand. Space like this, this near the coast, is not something you come across often.

title: Bright Ground-Floor Apartment a Short Walk to Papamoa Beach
description: Single-level living at its easiest, this tidy two-bedroom apartment will appeal to first-home buyers and anyone scaling back. The sunny open-plan layout connects to a private patio framed by established gardens, and the beach, bus routes, and local cafes are all close by. A heat pump and secure parking keep things practical and low-fuss. The result is a relaxed, uncomplicated lifestyle right where you want it.

title: Elevated Bethlehem Home with Harbour and Hinterland Views
description: Sitting on a quiet rise in Bethlehem, this four-bedroom home takes in a sweeping view across the harbour to the ranges beyond. Expansive glazing pulls that outlook deep into a sunny open-plan living space that opens to a wraparound deck. A double garage, ensuite, and dedicated study give the home real flexibility for a growing household. Zoned for popular schools, it is only minutes from the Bethlehem town centre.

title: Sunny Bethlehem Townhouse Backing onto Reserve Walkways
description: Backing straight onto leafy reserve walkways, this three-bedroom townhouse offers a peaceful setting without giving up town-centre convenience. The open living area drinks in all-day sun and connects to a private deck that looks over native bush. Double glazing, a heat pump, and internal-access garaging add comfort and security in equal measure. It is a serene, low-effort home set within a genuinely friendly community.

title: Quality Bethlehem Family Home in a Tightly Held Street
description: Homes in this quietly coveted Bethlehem street rarely change hands, which makes this well-presented four-bedroom residence one to watch. Generous, separate living spaces give everyone room to spread out, anchored by a sun-filled kitchen at the heart of the home. The fully fenced backyard keeps children and pets safe, with established gardens adding a welcome layer of privacy. Schools, the village, and the riverside trails are all an easy walk from the door.

title: Stylish City-Edge Apartment in the Heart of Tauranga
description: Set just moments from the waterfront, the restaurants, and the CBD, this contemporary two-bedroom apartment makes city-edge living simple. An efficient open-plan design pulls in the light and opens to a balcony with leafy city outlooks, and a sleek modern kitchen anchors the space. Secure parking and a tidy footprint make it equally appealing to professionals and investors. Everything Tauranga Central has to offer is quite literally at the door.

title: Restored Character Villa Walking Distance to the CBD
description: Full of period charm, this lovingly restored three-bedroom villa marries ornate ceilings and timber floors with carefully chosen modern updates. The sunny renovated kitchen opens to a private deck and a fully fenced garden alive with established plantings. The CBD, the cafes, and the harbour edge are all only a few minutes on foot. It is a home with genuine soul, a rare find this close to the centre of Tauranga.

title: Sun-soaked family home a short stroll from the Mount beaches
description: North-facing and full of light, this weatherboard home sits on a quiet street where the loudest thing you'll hear is the surf. The three bedrooms are properly sized, and the living room slides open onto a deck that catches the evening sun long after dinner. Kids and the dog get a flat, fully fenced lawn to themselves. You're a few minutes from the base track, Main Beach, and the Maunganui Road coffee crowd.

title: Low-maintenance modern townhouse moments from Mount hot pools
description: If you'd rather spend weekends at the beach than mowing lawns, this two-storey townhouse is built for you. The tiled downstairs lounge stays warm and bright, opening to a small courtyard you can actually keep on top of. Two double bedrooms upstairs share a tidy bathroom with good storage. The hot pools and Pilot Bay are a short drive away, making it an easy pick for a couple or busy professional.

title: Furnished sea-view apartment in the heart of the Mount
description: The view does the heavy lifting here: open the balcony door and the ocean stretches out in front of you. Inside, floor-to-ceiling glass keeps the living space bright, and everything you need is already in place, so you can arrive with a suitcase and start living. The complex is secure with lift access and a dedicated park. Swap the morning commute for a swim off Marine Parade.

title: Bright self-contained studio steps from Mount Main Beach
description: Small but smartly laid out, this studio gives a single tenant or couple their own private entrance, a neat kitchenette and a sunny corner to relax in. It's tucked behind a leafy garden, so it stays quiet despite being a two-minute walk to the sand. Good insulation and a heat pump keep it comfortable whatever the season. Trade square metres for the privilege of beach mornings before work.

title: Spacious four-bedroom family home in popular Papamoa
description: Room to breathe is the theme of this single-level home. The kitchen and dining run together into one big sociable space, then carry on out to a covered deck that gets used year-round. The master has its own ensuite and walk-in robe, with three more bedrooms down the hall. The section is fully fenced, and Papamoa Beach, the local primary and Fashion Island shops are all close by.

title: Brand-new three-bedroom townhouse near Papamoa Beach
description: Still smelling of fresh paint, this near-new townhouse hasn't put a foot wrong: soft-close drawers in the kitchen, double glazing throughout, and a low-fuss native garden that won't demand your weekends. Three bedrooms and two and a half bathrooms mean nobody's queueing in the mornings, and the internal-access garage earns its keep on rainy days. The dunes are a flat walk or a quick bike ride away.

title: Tidy three-bedroom home with double garage in Papamoa
description: A solid brick-and-tile home that's been looked after and it shows. There's a separate lounge for when you want quiet, plus an open family room off the kitchen for everyone else. The backyard is fully fenced and safe for the little ones and the dog. It's a quiet street, an easy run to the Gravatt Road shops, and a short drive to the beach reserves that make Papamoa what it is.

title: Character family home on a leafy Bethlehem street
description: There's something settled about this four-bedroom home, framed by mature trees and full of the kind of character newer builds can't fake. A wide north-facing deck looks over the fenced garden, and the heat pump and insulation keep the open living area cosy through winter. It's zoned for the Bethlehem schools families chase, with the village shops just up the road. A genuinely easy place to plant roots.

title: Warm two-bedroom townhouse in central Bethlehem
description: Designed to be simple to live in, this two-bedroom townhouse is double-glazed, well-insulated and soaks up the afternoon sun. The kitchen and living open onto a private courtyard that's just right for a morning coffee or a Friday barbecue. Best of all, the Bethlehem Town Centre is a short walk away, so the supermarket, cafes and cinema are never more than a few minutes from the door.

title: Stylish city apartment in the heart of Tauranga Central
description: City living without the hassle: this apartment looks out over the streets below, with floor-to-ceiling windows pulling light deep into the open living area. The kitchen has a dishwasher and enough bench space to host. With the waterfront, the Devonport Road shops and Tauranga's restaurants all on foot, the car can stay parked most of the week. A smart base for a professional who likes being in the thick of it.

title: Sunny ground-floor unit close to Tauranga Central CBD
description: Honest, private and easy to run, this ground-floor unit suits a couple or someone living solo who wants to be near everything. The lounge gets good sun, the kitchen is tidy, and the courtyard out back asks almost nothing of you. There's off-street parking, the hospital is close, and the Cameron Road bus routes are right there. Affordable central living without the apartment stairs.

title: Renovated three-bedroom home with harbour glimpses in Tauranga Central
description: Sitting up off the road, this freshly renovated home catches glimpses of the harbour from its sunny front rooms. The renovation kept what mattered, the original character, and added a brand-new kitchen and bathroom that feel right at home alongside it. The fenced section leaves room for a vege patch or a weekend project. You can walk to the CBD, Memorial Park and the waterfront, yet the street stays peaceful.

title: Sunny double room in relaxed coliving flat, minutes from Mount Maunganui beach
description: A bright north-facing double room in a tidy four-bedroom flat just a short stroll from the Main Beach and the base track walk. The shared living areas open onto a sheltered deck that catches the afternoon sun, perfect for summer barbecues with easygoing flatmates. Cafes, the surf and the weekend night market are all within walking distance, so you can leave the car at home.

title: Quiet single room for a working professional in a warm Papamoa home
description: Settle into a peaceful single room in a well-insulated home set back from the road in a friendly Papamoa cul-de-sac. The flat is heated throughout and double glazed, so winter evenings stay cosy without a big power bill. You are a five-minute drive from Papamoa Plaza and an easy bike ride to the long sweep of white sand that the suburb is famous for.

title: Room available in friendly girls-only flat near Tauranga CBD
description: A comfortable room in a clean, sociable girls-only flat right on the edge of the Tauranga Central waterfront precinct. Wander down to the harbour, the Saturday market and a string of good cafes in under ten minutes on foot. The current flatmates are students and young professionals who keep things tidy and welcoming, and the bus stop on the corner makes commuting simple.

title: Self-contained studio with own ensuite in leafy Bethlehem
description: Enjoy your own space in this private studio with a dedicated ensuite, tucked away at the rear of a modern family property in sought-after Bethlehem. The room has its own entrance and a kitchenette, giving you independence while sharing the lovely native garden and off-street parking. It is a quick drive to Bethlehem Town Centre and the schools, with the motorway link making the rest of the city easy to reach.

title: Affordable room in easygoing Papamoa flat, walk to the dunes
