description: A budget-friendly room in a laid-back coliving flat a few streets back from the Papamoa dunes and reserve. Flatmates here surf, run on the beach and share the odd Sunday roast, but everyone respects their own space too. The fully fenced section means pets are considered, and there is plenty of room to park the boat or bike out front.

title: Spacious boys-only room close to the Mount and the port
description: A generous room in a practical boys-only flat handy to the port, the gym and the Mount hospitality strip. The house is warm with a recently serviced heat pump and has a big shared garage for storing surfboards, tools and the weekly shop. It suits a tradie or shift worker who wants an uncomplicated, well-located base near everything Mount Maunganui has going on.

title: Bright room in a healthy, double-glazed Tauranga Central villa
description: This character villa has been brought up to a high standard with double glazing, HRV ventilation and full insulation, making it one of the warmest dry flats in Tauranga Central. The offered room is sunny and looks over a private courtyard, with a sociable kitchen and dining space at the heart of the home. You are walking distance to the hospital, the university campus and the cafes along Devonport Road.

title: Peaceful room with native garden outlook in family-friendly Bethlehem
description: A calm room overlooking established native plantings in a quiet pocket of Bethlehem, ideal for someone who wants a restful home base after work. Meals are included in the rent, with the owner cooking a shared dinner most weeknights, so it suits a busy professional new to the area. The walking and cycling tracks along the estuary are right on the doorstep and the city is a short drive across the bridge.

title: Tidy Top-Floor Apartment Steps from the Otago University Campus
description: This bright two-bedroom apartment sits on the top floor of an easy-care complex on the city fringe, with the campus, hospital and Botanic Garden all a flat few minutes' walk away. Open-plan living connects to a sheltered balcony, and a quiet heat pump takes the bite out of the southern winter. A proven rental record and genuine lock-and-leave simplicity make it an obvious pick for investors, while first-home buyers will appreciate stepping into a warm, low-fuss home in one of Dunedin's most walkable pockets.

title: Warm Brick Townhouse on a Quiet North Dunedin Street
description: Set back from the road on a peaceful street, this three-bedroom brick townhouse keeps daily life refreshingly simple, with the university and the Leith walkway both an easy amble away. Full insulation and double glazing hold the warmth in well past dark, and the courtyard garden asks for little beyond an occasional weekend tidy. Inside, a tidy modern kitchen and a single internal garage round out a sensible footprint that works equally well for a family, a flat of students, or a patient buy-and-hold investor.

title: Sun-Filled Family Home a Short Walk from St Clair Beach
description: Salt air and the muffled roll of the surf greet you each morning at this comfortable four-bedroom weatherboard home, a short stroll from the St Clair Esplanade, the hot saltwater pool and the cluster of village cafes. The open-plan living reaches out to a north-facing deck that stays sheltered when the southerly gets up, and a separate rumpus gives teenagers, hobbies or a home office their own corner. Out back, a fully fenced garden and a double garage tie together a genuinely relaxed slice of coastal Dunedin.

title: Elevated St Clair Home with Sweeping Ocean Views
description: Positioned high enough to claim an unbroken arc of the Pacific, this immaculately kept home draws the horizon straight into a sun-flooded living room and out across a generous entertainer's deck. Three double bedrooms, a smart ensuite and a stone kitchen keep entertaining effortless, while solid insulation and double glazing leave the southerly firmly outdoors. The beach, the golf links and the village dining strip are all an easy downhill wander, with the climb home rewarded by that view every single time.

title: Gracious Character Villa with City Views in Leafy Roslyn
description: Past a classic bullnose verandah, this elegant villa marries soaring stud heights and original rimu floors with a tastefully reworked kitchen and a sun-trap conservatory built for slow Sunday mornings. The elevated section looks out over the harbour and the city lights, yet Roslyn's village shops and cafes are only a level stroll along the ridge. Established camellias frame a fully fenced garden, completing one of the hill suburb's most quietly coveted addresses.

title: Renovated Family Home on a Sunny Roslyn Rise
description: A considered renovation has given this solid brick home an entirely new centre of gravity, with a designer kitchen opening to living and then onto a deck that holds the afternoon sun beautifully. Three good bedrooms, a walk-in wardrobe and a sharp ensuite make the everyday genuinely easy, while HRV ventilation and full insulation keep the air warm and dry through winter. Zoned for sought-after schools and a short walk from the village, it asks nothing more than that you move in.

title: Architectural Alpine Home with Remarkables Views in Queenstown
description: A wall of glass pulls the jagged Remarkables and the shifting blue of Lake Wakatipu deep into this striking cedar-and-schist home, purpose-built for the southern lakes way of life. Vaulted living opens onto a wraparound deck and a hot tub made for thawing out after a day on the mountain, while underfloor heating and triple-glazed joinery keep things snug at altitude. With the gondola, the lakefront and the ski-access roads only minutes away, it is a rare turnkey trophy in a blue-ribbon pocket of Queenstown.

title: Stylish Lakeview Apartment Walking Distance to Queenstown CBD
description: Glittering lake and mountain vistas pour through floor-to-ceiling glazing in this immaculate two-bedroom apartment, with Cecil and Walter Peak standing guard across the water. The open kitchen-living zone steps out to a sheltered balcony made for a glass at sundown, and secure basement parking plus lift access keep alpine living effortless year-round. The waterfront, the gondola base and the hum of the town centre all sit a gentle few minutes' walk downhill.

title: Near-New Townhouse Moments from Queenstown's Frankton Hub
description: Designed around easy southern-lakes living, this crisp three-bedroom townhouse pairs a sleek stone kitchen with a sunny upstairs master and ensuite, plus a courtyard that barely needs a weekend's attention. Double glazing and an efficient heat pump keep running costs sensible and the rooms warm right through the alpine winter. With the airport, Remarkables Park shopping and the lake all close to hand, it makes an equally tidy lock-and-leave base or a dependable holiday rental.

title: Schist Character Home Steps from Historic Arrowtown Village
description: Set on a quiet lane beneath a canopy that turns gold each autumn, this characterful schist-and-cedar home balances rustic warmth against a tasteful modern fit-out and a sun-soaked living wing. Mornings start with birdsong and a wander along the Arrow River trails, with the Buckingham Street cafes and galleries only minutes on foot. A cosy wood-burner, a sheltered courtyard and a double garage finish off a genuine piece of old-gold-town character that rarely changes hands.

title: Private Lifestyle Block with Mountain Outlook near Arrowtown
description: On a generous two-hectare holding of grazing paddocks beneath the Crown Range, this comfortable lifestyle home gives you room to spread out just minutes from the historic village. Open-plan living tracks the changing alpine light through the day, while a self-contained studio handles guests, a home office or a teenager after a little independence. Established shelter belts, a productive vege garden and plenty of shedding round it out into a rare chance at the southern lakes country life.

title: Light-Filled Family Home a Short Stroll from Lake Wanaka
description: Built to chase every hour of sun, this modern four-bedroom home runs from a high-spec scullery kitchen into an airy living space that opens to a fully fenced lawn and a sheltered entertainer's deck. The lakefront, the township cafes and the ski-field access roads are all minutes away, with the Clutha trails practically at the gate. Underfloor warmth, double glazing and a double garage make it a warm, fuss-free base for a family living the active Wanaka lifestyle.

title: Elevated Building Section with Lake and Mountain Views at Wanaka
description: A genuinely rare chance to secure a north-facing, fully serviced section in a tightly held new Wanaka neighbourhood, framing views across the lake to Treble Cone and the Buchanan Peaks. The gently sloping site is set up for an architectural build, with services already at the boundary and a building covenant in place to protect the outlook for years to come. Cycle trails, the township and the marina all sit a short ride away, leaving you free to design the alpine home you have always pictured.

title: Sunny three-bedroom family home steps from the Botanic Garden
description: Set on a quiet leafy street in North Dunedin, this weatherboard villa pairs character bones with sensible modern comforts. Polished native timber floors run through the open living area, which opens onto a north-facing deck made for lazy weekend brunches. With the university, the Gardens and Knox Church all within an easy stroll, it suits a settled student flat or a young family wanting walkability. A new heat pump and a fully fenced back lawn round out a genuinely warm, easy-care home.

title: Bright furnished two-bedroom apartment by the campus edge
description: Tucked into a tidy low-rise block on the fringe of North Dunedin, this first-floor apartment is move-in ready and toasty year round. The compact galley kitchen flows to a sunlit lounge, while both double bedrooms catch the morning light and stay quiet despite the central location. Fully furnished down to the bed linen and crockery, it is ideal for postgrads or visiting staff who want to unpack and start living. Buses, cafes and the Leith trail are all on the doorstep.

title: Beachside St Clair home with sea views and a sheltered deck
description: Wake to the sound of the surf in this elevated four-bedroom home perched above the St Clair esplanade. Floor-to-ceiling glass frames a sweeping view across the bay, and the sheltered west-facing deck holds the sun well into the evening. The open kitchen and dining zone is built for entertaining, with a wood-look engineered floor and a generous walk-in pantry. A short downhill wander takes you to the hot saltwater pool, the surf breaks and the strip of beachfront cafes.

title: Low-maintenance St Clair townhouse a block from the surf
description: This crisp two-storey townhouse offers easy coastal living without the upkeep of a big section. Downstairs is open and light, with a modern kitchen opening to a small private courtyard that is a sun trap on still afternoons. Upstairs the two doubles share a smart family bathroom, and the master enjoys a glimpse of the sea over the rooftops. Walk to the beach, grab a flat white on the esplanade, or cycle the flat run into town in under fifteen minutes.

title: Character Roslyn villa with city lights and a native garden
description: Perched on the Roslyn ridge, this three-bedroom villa trades on its outlook, capturing the harbour and the twinkling city below from the front rooms. Inside, high stud ceilings, sash windows and a tiled open fire give it real warmth and personality, while a recent reno added a tidy kitchen and double glazing. The terraced native garden is alive with tui and bellbird and needs little fuss. The Roslyn village shops, deli and the cable-car-era cafes are a flat stroll away.

title: Tidy two-bedroom Roslyn unit, warm and easy to run
description: A smart brick-and-tile unit in a quiet block, this one is all about comfort and low running costs. Both bedrooms are doubles with built-in robes, and the open living space stays snug thanks to a modern heat pump and full insulation. A flat, fully fenced rear yard gives kids or a dog room to roam, and there is a single garage plus extra off-street parking. Handy to the Roslyn shops, the supermarket and a quick commute down to the central city.

title: Spacious Queenstown family home with mountain outlook
description: Backing onto reserve in a sought-after Queenstown pocket, this four-bedroom home delivers space, sun and a knockout view of the Remarkables. The open-plan living spills onto a large deck that frames the ranges, perfect for an apres-ski wine after a day on the slopes. A double garage, separate laundry and a master with ensuite and walk-in wardrobe make it genuinely liveable for a family. Schools, the Frankton shops and the lakefront are all a short drive away.

title: Modern furnished one-bedroom apartment near the Queenstown waterfront
description: This stylish furnished apartment puts you minutes from the buzz of the Queenstown waterfront and the gondola. The open living area is finished in warm timber tones and opens to a juliet balcony with a slice of lake view, while underfloor heating keeps it cosy through the alpine winter. Designed for one or a couple, it comes fully kitted out and ready to live in. Restaurants, the trail network and the ski transfers are all within a few minutes walk.

title: Schist-clad Arrowtown townhouse moments from the historic village
description: A beautifully built townhouse in the heart of Arrowtown, clad in local schist that nods to the town's gold-rush heritage. Three bedrooms and two bathrooms are spread over two levels, with a double-height living room that floods with light and a log burner for crisp autumn evenings. The compact, fully fenced courtyard is paved for easy care. Stroll to the village's tree-lined main street, the river trail and the boutique eateries that make Arrowtown so loved in every season.

title: Quiet three-bedroom Arrowtown home with a sunny lawn
description: Set on a peaceful cul-de-sac, this single-level home offers relaxed Arrowtown living with all-day sun and an easy flat section. The light kitchen and dining area look out over a fully fenced lawn framed by mature deciduous trees that turn gold each April. A heat pump and good insulation keep things warm when the frosts roll in, and there is plenty of off-street parking for bikes, boards and trailers. The village, golf course and Lake Hayes loop are all close at hand.

title: Contemporary Wanaka home with lake and mountain views
description: A light-filled architectural home in a tightly held Wanaka neighbourhood, capturing the lake and the snow-dusted Treble Cone range from the main living level. The kitchen is a chef's delight with stone benches and a butler's pantry, opening to a sheltered deck for long summer evenings. Four bedrooms include a master with ensuite and walk-in robe, and the home is warm and dry with double glazing throughout. The lakefront, town centre and trail heads are a short ride away.

title: Cosy furnished studio in central Wanaka, walk to the lake
description: A neat self-contained studio just back from the Wanaka lakefront, ideal for a single professional or a seasonal worker who wants to be in the thick of it. The clever layout makes the most of every metre, with a tidy kitchenette, a warm heat pump and a private entrance off a sunny shared garden. It comes fully furnished and is genuinely walk-in ready. Pop down to the lake for a morning swim, grab a coffee in the village, then hit the trails or the ski road.

title: Sunny double room in warm North Dunedin flat, walk to campus
description: There's a generous double going in our settled student flat, two minutes from the Leith and an easy walk to the Otago campus. The lounge heat pump and double glazing mean you'll actually be warm come July, which is more than most North Dunedin flats can promise. Three of us live here already; we keep the kitchen sorted and the noise down on weeknights, but we're far from boring. When deadlines lift, the back deck catches the afternoon sun and that's usually where flat dinners happen.

title: Room available in tidy North Dunedin villa, great for postgrads
description: We're after a quiet postgrad or working professional to take a room in our character villa on the greener edge of North Dunedin. It gets lovely afternoon light, and since the walls were re-insulated and the HRV went in, the old damp problem is gone. Campus is a five-minute cycle and the Botanic Garden is close enough for a Sunday wander before the week starts again. Both current flatmates work long days, so the house tends to be calm, clean and easy to live in.

title: Bright room steps from the beach in coastal St Clair flat
description: If you've ever wanted to fall asleep to the surf, this St Clair flat sits a two-minute walk from the Esplanade and the hot saltwater pool. The room on offer is a good size and properly bright, and the heat pump takes the bite out of those brisk coastal starts. The current crew are keen on early ocean swims and long weekend coffees down the strip, so it suits someone who genuinely loves the sea. The deck looks out over the water, and the sunsets from there are something else.

title: Self-contained studio room in elevated Roslyn home with city views
description: This is a private studio room with its own ensuite, set in a modern Roslyn home that sits high above the city. The double glazing runs floor to ceiling, framing the harbour and the lights of Dunedin below, and there's a heat pump so the space holds its warmth right through winter. Roslyn village and the town bus are a short walk down the hill. The owner runs a spotless, quiet household and is hoping to find a mature flatmate who'll value the privacy as much as the view.

title: Cosy room in friendly Roslyn flat, handy to village and buses
description: A comfortable room has opened up in our Roslyn flat, and we'd love an outgoing person to fill it. Between the lounge heat pump and the fresh insulation right through the house, this is honestly one of the warmer flats up on the hill. The boutique cafes and bakeries of the village are a stroll away, with a quick bus down into the central city when you need it. The yard is fully fenced, so a well-behaved dog is welcome, and we do tend to put on a shared meal every week or so.

title: Room for rent in modern Queenstown flat with mountain outlook
description: A roomy double is free in this contemporary Queenstown flat, and the outlook over the Remarkables genuinely stops you in the morning. The place is built for alpine living, with a heat pump, double glazing and a lock-up garage that keeps your skis and bikes out of the frost. You're a short hop from the gondola, the lakefront and the centre of town, which makes it ideal for a season worker or anyone in hospo. We're an active bunch here, always game for a trail run or a quick dip in the lake after a shift.

title: Flatmate wanted for character Arrowtown cottage near the river
description: We've got a room going in our little Arrowtown cottage, set among the old schist buildings and the autumn trees the town is famous for. It's a warm, wood-toned place backed up by a heat pump, and the fully fenced garden opens straight onto the river trails. The pace here is slow in the best way, ideal for someone who's into cycling, a spot of fishing, or just quiet evenings, with Queenstown only a short drive over the hill. We're laid-back and happy to share the odd Sunday roast with the right person.

title: Spacious room in sunny Wanaka flat, minutes from the lakefront
description: A bright, roomy space is up for grabs in our modern Wanaka flat, just minutes from the water and the easy cafe scene in town. The flat really catches the sun, with a deck made for summer barbecues and a heat pump to see you through the sharp Central Otago cold snaps. For anyone who lives outdoors it's a perfect base, close to Treble Cone, the Clutha trails and tramping in every direction. The flatmates are a friendly mix of seasonal workers and locals who keep the place warm, tidy and genuinely welcoming.
